baseline.notes: Run 43 ceiling-break baseline: F1 0.409, P 0.422, R 0.397, TP 62. Run 47 on sporting_ac with YOLO crop validated the pixel-density hypothesis: coarse firing rate 3.1% -> 10.3%, F1 0.153 -> 0.215, TP 11 -> 19. Run 48 tests whether YOLO crop also holds or improves Rush, where baseline firing (22.8%) is already adequate. Rush has a smaller expected gain from cropping (camera already fills the frame with field), but also smaller regression risk. This run decides whether to ship the feature across all 4 games.

targets.notes: Primary criterion: Rush F1 must hold >=0.409. If Rush F1 improves or holds, ship YOLO crop and re-run the cross-game batch (Run 49) with the new config. If Rush regresses by >0.02 F1, the feature is a cross-game trade-off — return to the drawing board. Secondary: coarse firing rate on Rush (baseline 22.8%) should stay similar; a big move either way is a red flag.

yolo_crop_enabled.reason: YOLO tight-crop of each sampled frame to the bounding box of detected persons+ball. Validated on sporting_ac in Run 47 (firing rate 3x, F1 +0.062). Rush test determines whether the feature ships.

notes: Run #48 — Rush regression check with YOLO_CROP_ENABLED=true. Same commit c230b26 as Run 47. Worker is on afebe70 with the same code loaded. Expected smoke-test observations: Run 47 pre-flight showed YOLO model loaded 1s after start; similar behavior expected here.
